The instruction mentions 'Find an existing equality function in @tanstack/router-core' but n

no_solution_leakage.reason: Step 4 says 'Find an existing equality function in @tanstack/router-core that can compare objects deeply' which strongly hints at deepEqual. Combined with step 3 ('store references change even if values are equal') and step 2 ('useStore from @tanstack/react-store'), the instruction essentially tells the agent to add a deep equality comparator as argument to useStore calls. This is the fix, not just a description of what's broken.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: Tests assert gold-specific implementation details: test_deepequal_import_in_scripts requires exact import string 'import { deepEqual } from "@tanstack/router-core"', test_scripts_ast_usestore_has_deepequal counts 'deepEqual,' occurrences (>=2), test_headutils_ast_usestore_has_deepequal counts 'deepEqual,' occurrences (>=5). An alternative correct fix using a different equality function (e.g., custom isEqual, lodash isEqual, or inline comparison) would fail these tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: Tests test_deepequal_import_in_scripts, test_deepequal_import_in_headutils, test_scripts_ast_usestore_has_deepequal, and test_headutils_ast_usestore_has_deepequal are purely text-based checks (reading file content and checking for string patterns). While there are also execution tests (TypeScript compilation, unit tests, lint, build), the fail-to-pass tests are entirely string matching.

test_not_tautological.reason: The f2p tests (test_deepequal_import_in_scripts, test_deepequal_import_in_headutils, test_scripts_ast_usestore_has_deepequal, test_headutils_ast_usestore_has_deepequal) only check for string presence in files. Simply adding the import line and inserting 'deepEqual,' strings in the right places (without any actual behavioral change) would pass these tests. The pass-to-pass tests (types, unit, lint, build) don't verify the re-render optimization behavior either.

behavior_in_tests.reason: The instruction describes 'unnecessary re-renders' as the core problem, but no test actually verifies that re-renders are reduced. Tests only check for textual presence of deepEqual in source files and that existing CI checks pass.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive docstrings (e.g., 'AST-based verification that useStore calls in Scripts.tsx have deepEqual argument'), clearly separated into import checks, compilation checks, and AST-based verification.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task doesn't produce structured data like JSON/CSV/API/DB.

no_typos.reason: File paths, commands, variable names, and package names in instruction.md are all correct and consistent with the repository structure.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names exact files (Scripts.tsx, headContentUtils.tsx) and provides detailed investigation steps that essentially guide to the solution: 'Identify the store subscription mechanism used (useStore from @tanstack/react-store)' and 'Find an existing equality function in @tanstack/router-core that can compare objects deeply'.
